final_answer:
The exact connector should be a **standard DEX price adapter interface** sitting between staking and whichever DEX you choose later.

The structure is:

```text
Staking
-> PriceFeed
-> ActiveDexPriceAdapter
-> Chosen DEX on-chain price path
```

Staking only talks to `PriceFeed`.

`PriceFeed` only talks to the currently approved adapter.

The adapter is the part that knows the chosen DEX.

Example interface:

```solidity
interface IDexPriceAdapter {
    function getTwapPrice(
        address baseToken,
        address quoteToken,
        uint32 twapWindow,
        bytes calldata routeData
    )
        external
        view
        returns (
            uint256 price,
            uint256 liquidity,
            uint64 lastUpdatedBlock
        );
}
```

For Gold Chain:

```text
baseToken = wGILT
quoteToken = wGOLD
price = wGILT priced in wGOLD
```

If later you choose a ve(3,3) DEX, you build:

```text
Ve33DexPriceAdapter
```

If later you choose the current repo DEX, you build:

```text
CurrentDexPriceAdapter
```

Both expose the same function:

```text
getTwapPrice(...)
```

So staking does not change.

Dynamic governance setup:

```text
activeAdapter = adapter address
activeRoute = wGILT -> wGOLD path
twapWindow = 24h / 48h / 7d
minLiquidity = required liquidity
maxDeviation = max allowed price movement
activationEpoch = when change starts
```

Governance can later do:

```text
deploy new adapter
approve new adapter
set new route
set activation epoch
wait delay
switch PriceFeed to new adapter
```

The staking logic remains:

```text
price = PriceFeed.getGiltGoldPrice(epoch)
gilt_value = gilt_amount * price
gold_value = gold_amount
reward_weight = calculated from those values
```

Why this matters: the DEX can change later without rewriting staking.

What should be done next: define the fixed `IDexPriceAdapter` interface and make every future DEX connector implement that same interface.
